goal: Complete Phase 2b by adding a Study Plan page centered on Target Date, enforcing deep-review daily cap with priority-based sorting, and finalizing statistics/vocabulary management consistency including renaming all '考试日期' language to '目标日期/Target Date'.
scope: Study Plan page UI and state flow (Target Date inputs, daily plan summary, progress-to-target calculations)
Global copy and label normalization for date concept (replace any exam-specific wording with Target Date terminology)
Deep review daily cap logic and gating behavior
Priority sorter logic for review queue ordering
Statistics page alignment with Study Plan/deep-review cap metrics
Vocabulary management updates needed to support priority and cap-aware workflows
constraints: Use existing app architecture/components/state patterns; avoid major refactor or new backend service unless strictly required.
Do not introduce exam-specific assumptions or wording; date semantics must remain generic and goal-oriented.
Keep backward compatibility for existing localStorage/supabase progress data; no destructive schema changes.
Deep-review cap must be deterministic per local day boundary and must not block non-deep-review flows.
Scope is Phase 2b only; avoid implementing unrelated monetization/auth redesign/API rewrites.
All user-visible copy changes must be consistent across Setup, Settings, Statistics, and learning-related prompts.
acceptance_tests: Study Plan page is accessible and displays Target Date fields/summary correctly with valid and empty states.
All prior '考试日期' text is replaced with '目标日期' and/or 'Target Date' in UI and related user-facing messages.
Deep review can only process up to configured daily cap; when cap is reached, UI shows clear guidance and prevents additional deep-review starts.
Priority sorter places highest-priority review words first (e.g., error/uncertain/due-weighted) and ordering is stable/reproducible.
Statistics page reflects cap usage and Target Date-related planning metrics without breaking existing accuracy/XP/status metrics.
Vocabulary management remains fully functional (search/filter/sort/bulk status) and integrates correctly with priority/deep-review constraints.
Production build and core learning regression (guess→teach→spectrum→review/cloze) pass unchanged after Phase 2b integration.
implementation_steps: Audit current Phase 2 code paths to locate date labels, review pipeline entry points, and stats/vocab dependencies; define exact insertion points for Study Plan and cap logic.
Implement Study Plan page/state with Target Date model, including validation, persistence, and plan summary calculations.
Apply comprehensive copy migration from exam-specific wording to Target Date terminology in all user-facing surfaces.
Add deep-review daily cap counters/checks and wire gating/UX feedback at deep-review entry and completion points.
Implement priority sorter for review candidates and integrate it into quick/deep review queue construction.
Update statistics and vocabulary management displays so plan/cap/priority data are visible and behavior remains coherent.
Run build + regression checks, then finalize with targeted verification for rename consistency, cap enforcement, and queue ordering.
summary: Phase 2b will extend Phase 2 with a Target Date-driven Study Plan surface and robust review-control mechanics (daily deep-review cap + priority ordering), while polishing terminology consistency and preserving existing learning flow stability.
